problem_addressed: Missing instances in Earth Observation time series data due to satellite failure or cloud occlusion introduce input-level uncertainty that degrades regression performance while being commonly overlooked in existing approaches.
core_claim: Monte Carlo Concrete Temporal Dropout (MC-ConcTD) improves predictive performance and uncertainty calibration in EO time series regression by learning optimal dropout distributions rather than using fixed ratios.
key_contributions: Introduction of Monte Carlo Temporal Dropout (MC-TD) for input-level uncertainty quantification using predefined dropout ratios
Development of Monte Carlo Concrete Temporal Dropout (MC-ConcTD) that learns optimal dropout distributions via continuous relaxation
novelty: Applying dropout at the input level across time dimensions for uncertainty quantification in EO regression tasks, and making dropout ratios learnable rather than requiring manual tuning.
method_summary: MC-TD applies random temporal dropout masks drawn from Bernoulli distribution during inference with fixed ratio α. MC-ConcTD extends this using Concrete distribution to learn α as a parameter via continuous relaxation, both using Monte Carlo sampling (L=20) for uncertainty quantification.
method_components: Temporal Dropout (TD) with binary masks from Bernoulli distribution
Concrete Temporal Dropout (ConcTD) with learnable parameters via continuous relaxation
Monte Carlo sampling for uncertainty estimation
key_innovation: Using learnable dropout ratios through Concrete distribution to automatically optimize uncertainty quantification, eliminating costly manual ratio selection while improving model calibration.
assumptions: Input uncertainty propagates through the model to result in epistemic uncertainty
Dropout approximates Bayesian inference in neural networks
The dropout ratio can be optimized via gradient descent
theoretical_grounding: Based on Gal et al.'s demonstration that dropout approximates Bayesian inference and Valdenegro-Toro's relationship showing input uncertainty propagates to output uncertainty through stochastic sampling.
hyperparameters: Optimal dropout ratio around 0.1; MC-ConcTD dynamically adjusts dropout value
headline_result: MC-ConcTD achieves best R² of 0.79 on SwissYield dataset
ablations: Comparison of TD variants (MC-TD vs MC-ConcTD) and established UQ methods (MC-Dropout, VI)
improvement_over_baseline: MC-ConcTD improves R² by 0.01 on SwissYield (0.78→0.79) and 0.03 on PM2.5 (0.74→0.77) vs MC-Dropout
best_result: MC-ConcTD achieves best R² of 0.79 on SwissYield dataset
title: An Analysis of Temporal Dropout in Earth Observation Time Series for Regression Tasks
